race.name: Ski de She
race.tagline: Twenty-four kilometers of classic skiing for women only — Wisconsin's all-women Nordic marathon celebrating the growing community of female distance skiers in the upper Midwest.

vitals.aid_stations: Aid stations with warm drinks and snacks

climate.description: February in the Cable area benefits from Lake Superior's influence on the regional snowpack. Temperatures typically -16°C to -5°C. The Chequamegon National Forest area receives reliable snowfall and cold temperatures that preserve trail conditions.
challenges: Deep cold in February with significant wind chill potential
Northern Wisconsin weather can be unpredictable
Remote Northwoods location requires travel planning
Classic technique on rolling terrain demands good wax selection

course.primary: Classic-technique course on the trail system near Cable, Wisconsin — the same Northwoods region that hosts the American Birkebeiner. Rolling terrain through the Chequamegon National Forest. Well-groomed classic tracks through hardwood and pine forest with moderate hills.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through Northwoods forest
course.grooming: Good grooming — benefits from Birkie trail infrastructure
features: Women-only event — dedicated space for female Nordic skiers
Located in the Birkebeiner trail system near Cable, WI
Chequamegon National Forest — classic Northwoods landscape
Growing community of women in distance Nordic skiing
Same region as the American Birkebeiner

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The women-only format is the defining and most valuable feature — very few events offer this in Nordic skiing. Strong community score for building female participation. Benefits from the Birkebeiner trail system infrastructure. Shorter distance, limited competitive depth, and regional scope keep the overall score modest.

history.summary: Ski de She is a women-only cross-country ski marathon held near Cable, Wisconsin, in the heart of the American Birkebeiner trail system. The event was created to provide a dedicated space for women in distance Nordic skiing, addressing the underrepresentation of women in citizen ski marathons. Held in the Chequamegon National Forest, it benefits from the trail infrastructure built for the nearby American Birkebeiner.
notable_facts: Women-only event — one of very few women's-exclusive ski marathons in North America
Located near Cable, WI — heart of the American Birkebeiner trail system
Chequamegon National Forest provides the Northwoods setting
Addresses the growing participation of women in distance Nordic skiing
Classic technique only — honoring traditional skiing
Benefits from the Birkebeiner trail grooming and infrastructure
